calendar.matters:
Notice Of Intention To Move To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Attorney Robert Kane for David Tse the moving party.
Attorney Eamon E. Jafari by Court Call for Respondent Freedom West Homes Corporation, A California Corporation the opposing party.

After hearing arguments from both parties in the matter of THIRD PARTY DAVID TSE Notice Of Intention To Move To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Mr. Tse fails to cite any law or fact entitling him to relief under CCP 473, CCP 473.5 or based on extrinsic fraud. Additionally, Mr. Tse has no standing to bring this motion under CCP 663 as he failed to show immediate or pecuniary interest qualifying him as an "aggrieved party" under the circumstances of this case. Mr. Tse may bring his argument regarding inapplicability of the judgment in this case to him as an indispensable party in his own pending action or another action (collaterally attack). Save Our Bay, Inc. v. San Diego Unified Port Dist. (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 686. Whether Mr. Tse is bound by the judgment in this case and whether is he an indispensable party shall be addressed, if at all, in the Tse matter.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge A. James Robertson, II, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Ccp 438)
Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported.

Petitioner Darryl Stitt, Petitioner/moving party
No Apearance by Respondent

The Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER DARRYL STITT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Ccp 438) is GRANTED as requested. Non-opposition filed. Written order is signed and filed.
